channel.title: Release Notes on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Release Notes on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Customization and configurability improvements
item.description:
Copilot code review now supports a firewall, custom setup steps, and independent runner configurations. It also reads custom instructions from the head branch to make testing and validation easier.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — June update
item.description:
June 2026’s Visual Studio Copilot update focuses on usage visibility, a new trust layer for MCP servers, and the first C++ scenarios. The post emphasizes controls and clarity rather than new broad functionality.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework July 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
The post is a brief recap of July 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided content does not include version numbers, fixes, or platform-specific details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, June 2026 releases
item.description:
This changelog covers GitHub Copilot updates in Visual Studio Code across VS Code v1.123 through v1.127, released during June and early July 2026. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature or bug-fix details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Announcing etcd v3.7.0
item.description:
etcd v3.7.0 adds RangeStream for chunked range results, improves range/lease/watch performance, and completes the move off legacy v2store dependencies. It also includes protobuf dependency cleanup, Unix socket support, new metrics, and several upgrade-breaking removals.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Desktop 3.6: Worktrees and deeper Copilot integration
item.description:
GitHub Desktop 3.6 adds Git worktree support and expands GitHub Copilot integration for commit authoring and merge conflict resolution. It brings more of the Git workflow into Desktop.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Automatic Dependabot access to GitHub-hosted registries
item.description:
Dependabot can now read from private GitHub Packages registries without a personal access token when the package has granted repository access through “Manage Actions access.” This removes the need to configure a PAT for that dependency source.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions: Build custom images from custom images
item.description:
GitHub Actions custom images now support building one custom image from another. This adds flexibility for composing and managing image-generation pipelines for GitHub-hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Generated release notes credit you for Copilot pull requests
item.description:
Generated release notes now credit Copilot pull requests in the list of merged pull requests since the last release. This affects the automatically generated release notes output.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Read remote repository content with GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now includes two commands for reading repository content without cloning: gh repo read-file and gh repo read-dir. These let you inspect remote files and directories directly from the terminal.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming breaking changes for npm v12
item.description:
npm v12 will change the default behavior of npm install with security-related defaults. The post says these changes are already available as warnings in npm 11.16.0 and newer.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 5 is now available!
item.description:
The post announces .NET 11 Preview 5 and says it includes updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and other areas. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework June 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
This post is a brief recap of the June 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided content does not include specific version numbers, fixes, or security details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: aks: Release 2026-05-29
item.description:
AKS release 2026-05-29 rolls out Kubernetes 1.36 preview and patch updates for 1.35.5/1.34.8/1.33.12, with multiple security and component updates. It also adds Windows Server 2025 and Azure Container Linux GA, expands AKS Automatic and LocalDNS behavior changes, and includes several retirement/deprecation notices for older Windows and Flatcar node images.

  • Source: Release notes from AKS

item.title: ai: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | May 2026
item.description:
May 2026 Microsoft Foundry updates include trace-based evaluation for any agent on any cloud, new catalog models (Grok 4.3 and DeepSeek V4), GPT-5 Reinforcement Fine-Tuning at gated GA, and several platform and SDK additions. The post also notes GA for Managed VNET and Content Understanding improvements, plus Foundry Local updates and a new azure-ai-projects release.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Mixed Version Proxy Graduates to Beta
item.description:
Kubernetes 1.36 graduates the Mixed Version Proxy (feature gate UnknownVersionInteroperabilityProxy) to Beta and enables it by default. The feature now uses Aggregated Discovery, adds peer-aggregated discovery for unified API listings across control-plane nodes, and requires --peer-ca-file plus peer advertise settings for secure API server-to-server proxying.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework May 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
Microsoft published a May 2026 recap of servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided text does not include specific version numbers, fixes, or security details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: ai: Foundry Local 1.1: Live Transcription, Embeddings, and Responses API
item.description:
Foundry Local 1.1 adds live transcription, embeddings, a Responses API, a WebGPU plugin, and download cancellation. The update expands local model capabilities and adds controls for managing downloads.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: ai: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | April 2026
item.description:
April 2026 Microsoft Foundry updates include Foundry Local GA for local AI development, GPT-5.5 support with default Tier 5 and Tier 6 quota, and new tracing, monitoring, and evaluation features for agents. The release also adds CodeAct with Hyperlight for sandboxed code execution and SDK updates for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, .NET, and Java.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Moving Volume Group Snapshots to GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 promotes volume group snapshots to GA, with the API graduating to groupsnapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1. The feature lets CSI-backed storage drivers take crash-consistent snapshots across multiple PVCs using label selectors and restore workloads from those grouped snapshots.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot CLI now supports more models
item.description:
Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot CLI now supports additional model combinations. It can use a Claude-powered critic agent in GPT sessions, with support also extended for Claude-based sessions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, April releases
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code changelog covers VS Code releases v1.116 through v1.119 from April and early May 2026. The post notes that Copilot can now search by meaning.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Declarative Validation Graduates to GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 moves Declarative Validation for native types to GA and makes the DeclarativeValidation feature gate enabled by default. The framework replaces handwritten validation with +k8s: markers and validation-gen, improving consistency, maintainability, ratcheting behavior, and future OpenAPI/tooling integration.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — April update
item.description:
The April 2026 GitHub Copilot update for Visual Studio focuses on agentic workflows. It adds direct cloud agent sessions from the IDE, user-level support for custom agents, and a new Debugger agent for validation.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Fine-Grained Kubelet API Authorization Graduates to GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 makes fine-grained kubelet API authorization GA. KubeletFineGrainedAuthz is now always enabled, allowing RBAC to target specific kubelet subresources like nodes/metrics and nodes/stats instead of the broad nodes/proxy permission.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog
